summary: Gate evidence is genuine and current-head-bound: all four verification records (unit/typecheck/integration/e2e) are strict-head bound to fd9745a with dirty=false and pass, and bundle-fd9745a.json reports 46/46 with verified artifact_check. Independent re-run of the five listed test files passed 40/40. The workflow_state_regression not_applicable closure is honest — the source diff confirms the change is only two pure helpers (safeReaddir ENOTDIR classification and buildEvidenceItem field-precedence), neither a workflow state machine, so no state could go stale/over-green; the genuine robustness concerns are covered by parse_failure and evidence_lifecycle_regression, and the vacuous e2e keyword token was removed from the integration record. No fabricated state-machine test and no rubber-stamp of a real defect.

judgment_delta: Initial skepticism that a workflow_state_regression failure mode flagged needs_human_judgment could be closed as not_applicable without masking a real defect. Final pass: reading the actual diff proved both changed functions are stateless (error-code classification + object field precedence), so the failure mode genuinely does not apply; closure reasoning matches the code and the real robustness surface is covered by other honest evidence, all strict-head-bound to fd9745a with clean tree and reproducible passing tests.
